author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Storage now translates jj's "Concurrent checkout" cascade to typed errors at the boundary. `JjError::is_concurrent_write` matches `"Concurrent checkout"` / `"Failed to check out commit"` substrings at `crates/jjf-storage/src/jj.rs:80`. `commit_record_change` / `commit_memory_change` wrap raw jj failures as `Error::ConcurrentWrite { hint }`. `Storage::mutate` and `Storage::add_comment` retry once on the typed error with a re-read of bookmark state (crucial: `add_comment_once` re-reads `existing_comments` so the racer's append isn't clobbered). A jittered 15-50ms sleep before retry breaks the simultaneous-retry re-collision loop. Slug-claim creates fail fast; the post-failure probe upgrades to `SlugCollision` via `find_open_slug_collision` if the slug is now visibly taken.

The CLI's `concurrent_write` envelope is documented in `docs/cli-json.md` (exit 1, `details.hint`). The `slug_collision` upgrade path reuses the existing pre-flight variant — same envelope shape (`slug`, `conflicts_with`), so scripts already pattern-matching on `slug_collision` need no changes.

Tests: `crates/jjf-storage/src/jj.rs` unit tests pin detector against the literal QA-observed stderr plus each independent phrase. `crates/jjf/tests/concurrent_write.rs` spawns parallel process pairs racing the same slug / same comment slot; the typed-error invariant (no `jj_error` envelope ever escapes) is the pinned acceptance.

## Recommendation

Ship. 421/421 nextest run green (415 baseline + 6 new). Last of the four QA red-team bugs cleared; the asterinas migration path is no longer hand-holding agents through raw jj stderr on the dominant error shape.

## Confidence

medium. The translation + retry is sound and the typed-surface invariant is well-pinned. Two observations to surface: (1) the integration test couldn't reliably reproduce the actual "Concurrent checkout" failure — under local-process timing jj usually serializes the working-copy contention, producing a divergent-bookmark outcome rather than the conflict error. The detector-level unit test plus the typed-error invariant test cover what's reachable; the actual race shape from the QA repro is rare under test timing. (2) The divergent-bookmark race produces TWO commits with the same slug on the bookmark — that's the merge-resolver's domain, not this ticket's, but it suggests a follow-up: slug uniqueness should be enforced at the bookmark level, not just pre-flight.

## Open follow-ups

- Follow-up worth filing: under the divergent-bookmark race, two `jjf new --slug <s>` invocations can BOTH land their issue on different bookmark heads with the same slug. The next `jjf pull` / merge resolver should detect and surface this. (Not blocking — this ticket's scope was UX of the conflict error, not the divergence resolution.)
- No new tickets filed.
